description: A designed 'rogues-gallery' arrest card in ICE's recurring Tren de Aragua template (square format). A blue header bar carries the headline 'TdA ARREST' in large yellow/gold serif caps. The center is a blurred candid in-custody photo of a young man in a lime-green jail/intake top, flanked by an officer in dark blue with his back turned, in an institutional corridor. Overlaid at left in bold white sans-serif is the detainee's name across four lines: 'Elvis Duglas Duarn-Suniaga.' A red banner across the lower-center lists the charges in white caps: 'FUGITIVE, RECEIVING STOLEN PROPERTY, ASSAULT, FORCED ABDUCTION & DRUG POSSESSION.' A bottom blue footer bar shows the circular DHS seal with 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' (white) at left and 'Tren de Aragua' in gold serif at right. The card uses a most-wanted/criminal-card aesthetic to spotlight an alleged gang member and his charges; the target audience is the general public, building support for enforcement by emphasizing violent crime and gang affiliation.
notable_text: TdA ARREST; Elvis Duglas Duarn-Suniaga; FUGITIVE, RECEIVING STOLEN PROPERTY, ASSAULT, FORCED ABDUCTION & DRUG POSSESSION; Tren de Aragua
